flashcards.front: What is the primary function of the physical layer?
flashcards.back: Transmit raw bit streams over physical media, handling electrical/optical signaling and hardware interfaces.

flashcards.front: Name two key characteristics of transmission media.
What distinguishes guided from unguided media?
flashcards.back: Guided uses physical path (cables), unguided uses electromagnetic waves through air/space.

flashcards.front: Why is twisted pair cable twisted?
flashcards.back: To reduce electromagnetic interference from external sources and crosstalk between pairs.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of fiber optics over copper?
flashcards.back: Higher bandwidth, longer distances, and immunity to electromagnetic interference.

flashcards.front: What frequency range do microwave transmissions use?

flashcards.back: Unshielded Twisted Pair - most common Ethernet cable type.

flashcards.front: What is modal dispersion in fiber optics?
flashcards.back: Signal distortion caused by different light paths (modes) traveling at different speeds.

flashcards.front: What is the typical maximum length for UTP CAT6 cable?
What are the two main types of optical fiber?
What makes coaxial cable more shielded than UTP?
flashcards.back: Concentric layers: central conductor, dielectric insulator, braided metal shield, plastic jacket.

flashcards.front: What is the primary use of radio waves in networking?
